Local Market Data

Where's the Middle TN Market Right Now?

Real numbers for the communities Edward serves — from affordable Clarksville to Murfreesboro's growing suburbs to Nashville's urban core.

Median Home Price
$480–500K
▲ 5% projected 2026
Condo Median
$350K
Steady demand
Active Listings
11,406
▲ 13% YoY — most since 2014
Days on Market
62–85 days
More time to decide
Sale-to-List Ratio
97–98%
Strong but negotiable
Projected Appreciation
3–5%
2026 annual forecast
🏠

Buyer's window open: Increased inventory means you have real choices and negotiating power for the first time in years. Sellers are offering closing cost help & rate buydowns.

📈

Values holding strong: Despite the cooldown, Nashville prices are 80%+ above pre-COVID levels. No crash — a healthy reset to sustainable growth.

🏢

Corporate anchor: Oracle, Amazon, and Alliance Bernstein relocations continue to fuel job growth and rental demand across the Nashville metro.

💡

Rate milestone: Mortgage rates recently dipped below 6% for the first time in 2 years, re-engaging buyers and sparking a 15% jump in pending sales nationally.

Bottom line for Nashville: The market has shifted from a seller's sprint to a strategic environment where buyers can do their homework and negotiate real terms. Sellers who price realistically still get strong results — Edward's 97.7% sale-to-list ratio is proof. This is a great window whether you're buying, selling, or investing.
Median Sale Price
$427K
▲ 1.7% YoY
Price Per Sq Ft
$214
Stable YoY
Days on Market
71 days
Up from 51 days last yr
Avg Offers
~2 offers
Still competitive
Hot Homes
33 days
Priced-right homes move fast
YoY Price Growth
+4.2%
Top 10 TN market
🌱

Steady riser: Murfreesboro is one of the top-performing markets in the state with +4.2% YoY growth, outpacing many larger metros.

🏘

Suburb strength: Mount Juliet, Smyrna, and Lebanon — all within the Murfreesboro orbit — continue drawing buyers priced out of Nashville proper.

⏱

Still somewhat competitive: Homes average ~2 offers. Priced-right listings still close in about 33 days — staging and strategy matter.

🎓

MTSU anchor effect: Middle Tennessee State University keeps rental demand and population growth strong year-round in Rutherford County.

Bottom line for Murfreesboro: A balanced market with genuine appreciation. More affordable than Nashville proper with strong fundamentals — great for first-time buyers who want to be close to the action without the Nashville price tag. Investors take note: MTSU-area rental demand stays steady.
Median Sale Price
$304K
▲ 1.3% YoY
Avg Home Value
$300K
Up 1.3% past year
Days on Market
85 days
Buyer-friendly pace
Homes Sold (Dec '25)
313
▲ vs 280 last yr
Cost vs National
−7%
Below avg cost of living
Projected Growth
~4.7%
Through Dec 2025 baseline
💰

Most affordable entry point: At $304K median, Clarksville is 29% below the national median — the best value play in Middle Tennessee for first-time buyers.

🪖

Fort Campbell drive: Military presence through Fort Campbell creates consistent year-round buyer & renter demand, making Clarksville a reliable investment market.

📦

Sales picking up: Home sales increased meaningfully year-over-year in late 2025, signaling renewed buyer confidence as rates dropped.

🚗

Nashville commuter hub: Just 45–60 minutes to Nashville, Clarksville draws buyers who want more house for the money while staying connected to the metro.

Bottom line for Clarksville: The most accessible door into Middle Tennessee homeownership. Whether you're a first-timer, a military family, or an investor looking for reliable cash flow, Clarksville checks the boxes — strong rental demand, below-average cost of living, and steady growth.
TN Median Sale Price

TN Homes For Sale

Sale-to-List Ratio
96.8%
Redfin Jan 2026
Avg Days on Market
88 days
+12 days YoY
5-Year Value Growth
+63%
Long-term strength
🇺🇸

Top relocation state: Tennessee is a top-5 destination for people moving from CA, NY, IL, WA, and MD — fueling sustained housing demand.

📊

Balanced conditions: 6 months of supply signals a balanced market. Both buyers and sellers can reach their goals with clear expectations.

🔑

Rate unlock effect: Rates below 6% are beginning to release sellers who were "locked in" at 3% rates — more inventory expected through 2026.

🌍

No-income-tax advantage: Tennessee's tax environment continues drawing out-of-state buyers and remote workers, keeping demand fundamentally strong.

The big picture: Tennessee's housing market is recalibrating — not collapsing. Prices have held, inventory is growing, and rates are finally moving in buyers' favor. For sellers, well-priced homes still close strong. For buyers and investors, Middle Tennessee's fundamentals — population growth, job expansion, and tax climate — are as compelling as ever.

Your Agent

Edward Stevenson, II

Realtor · Kijiji Realty · Nashville, TN

Real estate, for me, has always been about more than buying and selling homes — it's about building community and creating opportunity. With 9 years in the business, I've had the privilege of helping families, especially first-time homebuyers, take that first step into ownership.

There's nothing like seeing someone go from renting to owning and realizing they're not just buying a home — they're building generational wealth. I also work closely with investors because I'm one myself. Being part of an investment group gives me a deeper understanding of how to spot opportunities and think long-term.
